//: ArchDiagram launcher configuration. Edit the values below, then run ArchDiagram.cmd.
//source: Pick ONE source. If GitLabUrl is non-empty it wins; otherwise ProjectPath is scanned.
//clone: Where GitLab repos are cloned to (relative paths resolve against this launcher folder).
//output: Where the generated site lands (relative paths resolve against this launcher folder). Leave blank to auto-name it 'site-<project>' so sites for different sources can coexist here.
//display: MaxNodes caps how many nodes any single diagram shows before collapsing the rest.
//complexity: Set false to hide complexity reference points (rankings, badges). Snippets false hides inline source of complex methods.
//wiki: Set false to skip the offline wiki export (Confluence Storage Format) written to <output>/wiki.
//exclude: Optional list of directory NAMES to skip while scanning (matched anywhere in the tree), e.g. [ "node_modules", "dist" ]. bin/obj are already ignored by the scanner.
//sourcelink: Optional. Link files/nodes back to source. SourceLinkType is github|gitlab|local. SourceLinkBase is the repo web URL (https://github.com/org/repo) or a local root (C:/src/app). SourceLinkRef is the branch/tag (ignored for local). Leave Type blank to bake no links in; the viewer can still prompt for them in-browser (stored per browser).
//landscape: Set true (or run Launch-ArchDiagram.ps1 -Landscape) to skip scanning and instead cross-reference every already-generated site-*/model.json in this folder into a parent viewer at site-landscape.
//groups: Optional. When Groups is a non-empty array the launcher IGNORES ProjectPath/GitLabUrl/Landscape and instead: for each group, scans every project into its own site-<project> folder, then builds ONE landscape for that group at site-landscape-<group>. Set OverallLandscape true to also build a combined landscape of every project across all groups at site-landscape. A Projects entry is either a local path string, or an object {"GitLabUrl":"...","GitRef":"..."}. Re-run ArchDiagram.cmd periodically to keep everything up to date.
